mnemonics.title: MUX SELECTION FORMULA
mnemonics.content: Remember: SELECT LINES = LOG2(INPUTS). Think: SQuare ROOT gives SELECTION. If 4 inputs, need 2 select because 2^2 = 4. THE SQUARE OF SELECT LINES EQUALS INPUT COUNT.

mnemonics.title: MUX OPERATION
mnemonics.content: S = 0 GIVES OUTPUT 0 (I0), S = 1 GIVES OUTPUT 1 (I1). Think: ZERO-ONE, ZERO-ONE - S selects which GOES.

mnemonics.title: 2 TO 1 MUX FORMULA
mnemonics.content: Y = I0.(NOT S) + I1.S - Think: I ZERO NOT S + I ONE S. The input that matches the SELECT line value gets passed.

acronyms.usage: A device that selects one of multiple inputs and forwards it to a single output
A device that takes one input and routes it to one of multiple outputs
Control input that activates or deactivates the MUX operation
Communication technique that uses MUX to combine multiple signals into one channel
Control input that determines which data input is routed to output

visualTips.visualization: Think of a MUX like a rotary switch that connects one of many input wires to a single output wire. The select lines rotate the switch to point to the desired input.
Select lines act like a house address - they tell the MUX which INPUT 'house' to pick and send its data to the OUTPUT 'door'.
A larger MUX looks like a decision tree - first level selects groups, second level selects within groups. Each level uses one select bit.
Each AND gate acts as a guard at each input door. Only the guard whose select combination matches gets to open (output 1), passing that input through.

analogies.analogy: A MUX is like a train station with multiple platform tracks (inputs) but only one track leading out (output). The station master (select lines) decides which platform train goes to the exit.
Think of a TV remote channel selector - multiple channels (inputs) but one screen (output). The channel buttons (select lines) choose which program displays.
A MUX is like a valve system with multiple water pipes (inputs) connected to one outlet pipe. The valve handle (select lines) determines which pipe connects to the output.
A DEMUX is like a postal sorting facility - one mail stream (input) gets sorted to different delivery routes (outputs) based on address codes (select lines).
